description: A cylinder of wax with a wick that burns to give light; portable flame. 'Philu' names the living light—wax becoming warmth and glow.
sound_symbolism: The word flickers warm. 'Phi' /ɸi/ breathes with gentle flame, fire dancing. Then 'lu' flows with warm light, illumination spreading.
grammatical_notes: Functions as noun: 'philu' (the candle). More intimate than 'luma' (lamp). For meditation, ceremony, quiet evening.

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Candle as practice—single flame focusing attention, meditation object, impermanence visible.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Candles marked time—burning at known rate, measuring hours in darkness.
